purpose: Implement a local-first desktop point-of-sale / business app for a single-location small business, reusing a fixed set of primitives. The reference implementation is a tire shop; this spec generalizes it to other domains (hair salons, barber shops, detail shops, flower shops, and similar service+product businesses).
howToUse: Read this file top to bottom before writing code.
Build the domain-agnostic CORE exactly as specified (architecture, primitives, constraints).
Then apply ONE domainPack to customize branding, categories, and seed data. Do not fork the core per domain — only the domainPack changes.
Honor every entry in 'constraints' — each one encodes a real bug that was fixed in the reference implementation.
Run the 'verification' steps before declaring done.
'constraints' is a LIVING LOG of real bugs. When you hit and fix a non-obvious issue, append a new entry (id + rule + why) so the next instantiation cannot repeat it.

architecture.pattern: Local-first desktop app = Electron app shell + a Deno HTTP backend running as a localhost sidecar process + a single-file SQLite database. Fully offline; no cloud, no accounts.

processes.name: electron-main
responsibilities: Create the BrowserWindow (contextIsolation: true, nodeIntegration: false).
Pick a free 127.0.0.1 port, spawn the Deno backend as a child process, pass POS_PORT and POS_DB_PATH via env.
Poll GET /health until ready, then load the UI.
Expose a minimal IPC bridge for OS-only actions (print, save-PDF).

processes.name: backend
responsibilities: HTTP API bound strictly to 127.0.0.1 (never a public interface).
SQLite persistence via node:sqlite (built into Deno; no native build).
All money math and aggregation server-side.

processes.name: renderer
responsibilities: Touch-friendly UI (large targets, big type) for non-technical staff.
Talks to the backend over http://127.0.0.1:<port> via fetch.
Calls the preload bridge for print / save-PDF.

boundaries: renderer -> backend: plain HTTP over loopback (runtime-agnostic, cloud-portable later).
renderer -> electron-main: a tiny contextBridge surface (window.app) for OS actions only.
architecture.packaging: deno compile the backend into a single self-contained exe; electron-builder bundles it as an extraResource and produces a Windows NSIS installer. Target PC needs neither Deno nor Node installed.

frontend.charts: hand-rolled inline SVG (NO chart library — keep it offline and dependency-free)

constraints.rule: Store and compute all money as integer cents. Never use floats for currency.
constraints.why: Avoids floating-point rounding errors in totals/tax.

constraints.rule: The backend recomputes subtotal/tax/total from line items and the stored tax rate on every create. Never trust totals sent by the client.
Store created_at as UTC ISO for display, but also store a separate sale_date = local YYYY-MM-DD and group/report by sale_date.
constraints.why: Grouping by the UTC slice of created_at drops evening sales from 'today' in negative UTC offsets. Use a localDateString() helper for both writing sale_date and computing 'today'.

constraints.rule: The deno.json build:backend task MUST pass --no-npm and set "nodeModulesDir": "none".
constraints.why: Otherwise deno compile detects the Electron package.json and embeds the entire node_modules tree into the backend exe (hundreds of MB).

constraints.rule: The Windows backend exe MUST be compiled with deno compile --no-terminal, and Electron MUST spawn the packaged sidecar with windowsHide:true and stdio:'ignore' (dev may keep stdio:'inherit').
constraints.why: Without these, launching the installed POS also opens a visible backend console window, which looks broken to staff and invites accidental closure of the sidecar.

constraints.rule: Compile electron/ (main + preload) as CommonJS, and write a dist-electron/package.json containing {"type":"commonjs"} during the build. The root package.json is "type":"module".
constraints.why: Electron's sandboxed preload cannot be an ES module; an ESM preload silently fails to load, leaving window.app undefined (print/save-PDF break).

constraints.rule: For backups, do NOT use 'VACUUM INTO' (unsupported by node:sqlite — attached-db limit is 0). Instead run PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) then copy the db file.
constraints.why: node:sqlite limitation; checkpoint+copy yields a consistent standalone snapshot.

constraints.rule: Schema changes are additive and idempotent: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, plus an addColumnIfMissing(table,col,type) helper that checks PRAGMA table_info before ALTER. Backfill new columns. Never drop/rewrite existing data on upgrade.
constraints.why: Installed copies already hold the business's live data.

constraints.rule: Bind the backend to 127.0.0.1 only. Pick a free ephemeral port at launch; the renderer learns it via a ?port= query param and/or the preload bridge.
constraints.why: Security; never expose the API to the network.

constraints.rule: contextIsolation: true, nodeIntegration: false. Expose only an explicit, minimal API via contextBridge (e.g., window.app = { backendBaseUrl, printDoc, savePdf }).
Render charts/sparklines as inline SVG. Do not add a charting dependency.
constraints.why: Keeps the app fully offline and the bundle small.

constraints.rule: Inject the package.json version at build time (Vite define __APP_VERSION__) and show it subtly in the header + a Settings 'About' line. Bump package.json version and add a CHANGES.md entry for every shippable change.
constraints.why: Lets staff/support identify the running build; keeps in-place installer upgrades clean (NSIS upgrades require an equal-or-higher version).

constraints.rule: Set "base": "./" in vite.config.ts so the built index.html references ./assets/... (relative), not /assets/... (absolute).
constraints.why: The packaged app loads index.html over the file:// protocol. Absolute /assets/ paths resolve to the filesystem root and 404, leaving a blank window. Add did-fail-load logging in electron/main.ts so a load failure is never silent.

constraints.rule: Any static asset the BACKEND serves (logo, images) must be embedded as a base64 data URI compiled into the exe — generate a TS module (e.g. backend/logo.ts via scripts/embed-logo.mjs) and import it. The RENDERER imports the same file as a normal Vite asset (import logoUrl from './assets/x.jpg').
constraints.why: The backend ships as a single 'deno compile' exe with no asset files on disk, so it cannot read a logo from the filesystem at runtime. Inlined data URIs also avoid async load races during print()/printToPDF().

constraints.rule: Receipts that use color (brand bars, logos) MUST set '-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;' and an '@page { size: 80mm auto; margin: 0 }'. Electron's webContents.print() has NO preview pane, so provide an in-app preview by rendering the receipt HTML in a sandboxed <iframe srcDoc=...> with Print / Save PDF buttons.
constraints.why: Without print-color-adjust, Chromium drops background colors when printing/exporting. Staff expect to see the receipt before printing; webContents.print() jumps straight to the OS dialog, so the iframe IS the preview (and gives the browser's native preview during dev).

constraints.rule: A new settings key must be added in THREE places: (1) seed() defaults in db.ts, (2) the ALLOWED set in routes/settings.ts, and (3) a code-level fallback default where it is consumed (e.g. receipt.ts). Never assume the key exists.
constraints.why: seed() only runs when the settings table is EMPTY, so already-installed DBs never receive the new key. The consuming code must fall back to a default until the user saves Settings; the allow-list gate silently drops keys it doesn't recognize.

constraints.rule: node:sqlite .all()/.get() return Record<string, SQLOutputValue>. Cast typed rows through unknown first: 'stmt.all() as unknown as Row[]'. Coerce money columns with Number() at the boundary if doing arithmetic.
constraints.why: TypeScript rejects a direct cast from the SQLOutputValue record to a named interface ('neither type sufficiently overlaps'). Casting via unknown is the intended escape hatch.

constraints.rule: Tax is per-line, not per-sale. catalog_items and sale_line_items carry a taxable (0/1) column (added via addColumnIfMissing, default 1). computeTotals() sums the tax base over taxable lines ONLY, then applies the single tax_rate. Snapshot taxable into sale_line_items at sale time.
constraints.why: Oklahoma taxes tangible retail goods but exempts most personal services (haircuts, beard trims, salon services). A flat rate over the whole subtotal over-charges tax on exempt service lines, which is a real compliance problem for barber/salon/detail domains. A mixed cart (taxable retail + exempt service) must tax only the retail portion.

scripts/: embed-logo.mjs (bakes the logo into backend/logo.ts as a base64 data URI + copies it to renderer/src/assets/)
electron/: main.ts (window + sidecar spawn + health poll + print/savePdf IPC; packaged sidecar uses windowsHide:true + stdio:'ignore'; did-fail-load logging + POS_DEBUG devtools)
backend/: db.ts (node:sqlite Database class: schema, migrations, seed, queries, dashboard(), backupDaily(); exported helpers computeTotals, localDateString, dateMinusDays)
renderer/: src/App.tsx (tab nav + header with logo + version)
src/components/ (ReceiptPreview.tsx — iframe receipt preview reused by Checkout + Dashboard)
src/pages/ (Register.tsx — may be renamed per domainPack e.g. Checkout.tsx, Dashboard.tsx, Catalog.tsx, Settings.tsx)

dataModel.settings: key/value TEXT: business_name, business_address, business_phone, tax_rate (fraction string, e.g. '0.08625'), receipt_footer (multiline text printed at the bottom of receipts; \n preserved via white-space:pre-line). Seeded with domain defaults. New keys follow the 'additive-settings-keys' constraint.
dataModel.catalog_items: id, name, category, price_cents, active (0/1, soft-hide), color (hex|null, button color), description (text|null). Soft-delete sets active=0; permanent delete removes the row.
dataModel.sales: id, created_at (UTC ISO), sale_date (local YYYY-MM-DD), subtotal_cents, tax_cents, total_cents, payment_type ('cash'|'card'), cash_tendered_cents (nullable), change_cents (nullable). NOTE: 'sales' is the generic transaction table; keep the table name even if the domain calls them 'tickets', 'orders', or 'visits'.
dataModel.sale_line_items: id, sale_id (FK, ON DELETE CASCADE), name, unit_price_cents, qty, line_total_cents. Stores a SNAPSHOT of name/price so editing or deleting a catalog item never alters historical transactions.

api.desc: Update allowed settings keys (validate tax_rate in [0,1]).
Create a transaction; server recomputes totals; transactional insert of sale + lines.
Transactions for a local day + cash/card totals.
KPIs (revenue, count, avg ticket, items), prior-period comparison, daily series, recent list.

coreFeatures.register: Tap catalog buttons (colored by item.color, grouped by category, filterable by category tabs) or add a custom line item; adjust qty; Cash (amount tendered -> change due) or Card (recorded; charged on a separate terminal); complete -> persist -> print/save-PDF receipt -> clear.
coreFeatures.dashboard: Dark 'command center': range toggle (Today/7d/30d), animated count-up KPI cards with vs-prior-period delta chips, inline SVG revenue sparkline, cash/card mix bar, recent transactions list with reprint, polished empty state.
coreFeatures.catalog: Manage items: name, category (preset dropdown + custom), price, button color, description/note, Available toggle (hide/show), and permanent delete. Lists inactive items too.
coreFeatures.settings: Business name/address/phone (printed on receipts; name shown in header), editable tax rate, About/version line.
coreFeatures.receipts: Render branded receipt HTML: embedded logo header (data URI, see 'embed-binary-assets'), line items with qty×unit subline, subtotal/tax, a filled brand-color TOTAL bar (print-color-adjust), a Cash/Card badge + change, and an editable receipt_footer. Reviewed in an in-app iframe preview (see 'print-color-fidelity'), then Print via OS dialog or Save as PDF — no hardware required.
coreFeatures.backups: On startup, write a consistent daily snapshot to <db-dir>/backups/<app>-YYYY-MM-DD.db (checkpoint + copy), retain newest ~14, prune older. Never block startup on backup failure.

domainModel.principle: The core is a universal 'catalog of priced things -> cart -> paid transaction -> receipt -> dashboard'. EVERY listed domain maps onto it without schema changes. Customize only: branding/settings defaults, category presets + colors (catalog-meta.ts), seed catalog items, and optional UI relabeling (e.g., 'Register' -> 'Checkout').
customizationPoints: backend/db.ts seed(): settings defaults (business_name/address/phone, tax_rate, receipt_footer) + SEED_CATALOG items.
renderer/src/catalog-meta.ts: CATEGORIES preset list + COLORS palette.

Brand accent color: renderer/src/index.css --brand-from/via/to CSS vars — drive .bg-brand/.text-brand (header, CTAs, count-up totals, active pills) and the receipt TOTAL bar. Sweep any literal pink-/violet- utilities to match.
Logo: run scripts/embed-logo.mjs to bake <logo>.jpg into backend/logo.ts (data URI for receipts) AND copy it to renderer/src/assets/ for the header (imported in App.tsx).
package.json: name, description, build.appId, build.productName, build.nsis.shortcutName.
Optional: tab labels in App.tsx (e.g. 'Register' -> 'Checkout') and receipt wording in receipt.ts.

hair_salon.notes: Tips are common in salons — if needed, add an optional tip_cents column to sales via addColumnIfMissing and a tip step at checkout. Appointments are OUT of core scope; keep POS-only unless explicitly requested.

flower_shop.notes: Delivery is just a catalog item/line; no special handling needed for the core.

barber_shop.notes: Like hair_salon, tips are common — if needed add an optional tip_cents column via addColumnIfMissing and a tip step at checkout. Walk-in only; appointments are OUT of core scope unless explicitly requested.

npmScripts.dev: concurrently vite + (tsc electron -> write-electron-cjs.mjs -> wait-on 5173 -> electron .)

releaseOrder: bump package.json version + add CHANGES.md entry

backend: deno test passes: tax math + server-side total recompute; cash change-due; configurable tax rate; catalog soft-delete vs permanent; sale_date set to local day and returned by listSalesForDay(localToday); migrations add+backfill columns on an old-schema DB; backupDaily writes one snapshot/day, prunes, and is a no-op for :memory:; dashboard today/7d/30d sums, prev windows, daily series lengths (7/7/30), items_sold, avg_ticket, empty range has no divide-by-zero; HTTP shapes for /sales and /sales/dashboard.
Live: run backend, POST a sale, GET /sales/dashboard?range=today and confirm values + that a backups/ file appears.
renderReceipt() output embeds the logo data URI (data:image/...) and the receipt_footer text; PUT /settings accepts receipt_footer; the TOTAL bar markup is present.
frontend: npm run build:renderer type-checks and bundles clean; __APP_VERSION__ is baked into the bundle; the built index.html references ./assets/... (relative base) and the logo asset is emitted under dist-renderer/assets/.
shell: After packaging, on the target OS: the UI renders (NOT a blank window — confirms vite base './'), window.app is defined (preload loaded), Save PDF opens the OS dialog and writes a valid PDF, Print renders the formatted receipt with its background colors intact (not the whole window).
endToEnd: Ring a sale -> receipt prints/saves; Dashboard KPIs animate and toggle Today/7d/30d; data persists across restart; the daily backup file exists.

implementationSteps: 1. Scaffold configs (package.json, deno.json with the constraints above, vite/tailwind/postcss/tsconfig, electron/tsconfig.json as CommonJS, scripts/write-electron-cjs.mjs, .gitignore).

2. Backend db.ts: Database class with schema, addColumnIfMissing migrations, seed (domainPack), computeTotals, localDateString, dateMinusDays, createSale (transactional, server-side totals, sale_date), catalog CRUD + soft/permanent delete, listSalesForDay, dashboard(range), backupDaily().
4. server_test.ts covering the verification list; run deno test.
5. Electron main.ts (free port, spawn sidecar, health poll, print/savePdf IPC) + preload.ts (contextBridge window.app). Compile CommonJS + marker.

6. Renderer: api.ts, types.ts, catalog-meta.ts (domainPack categories/colors), useCountUp, pages (Register, Dashboard, Catalog, Settings), App.tsx nav + header (shop name + version), Vite define __APP_VERSION__.
7. Apply the chosen domainPack (branding, seeds, categories). Build, test, package.
8. Bump version + CHANGES.md; produce the installer.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs fully offline on one PC; data in a single SQLite file under the OS user-data dir.
Switching domains required edits ONLY to the domainPack customization points — the core code is identical across domains.
deno test green; renderer builds clean; installer produced; Save PDF / Print work via the preload bridge.
Money correct to the cent; daily totals correct in the evening (local-day grouping); automatic daily backups present.
referenceImplementation: Rivera Tire POS is the canonical example of the tire_shop domainPack. Jaretzy Hairsalon POS is a proven second instantiation (hair_salon domainPack) that contributed the later 'constraints' — vite-relative-base, embed-binary-assets, print-color-fidelity, additive-settings-keys, node-sqlite-row-typing — plus the brand-color tokens, embedded logo, branded receipt, and in-app receipt preview. Mirror this structure and conventions.
